terms.definition: Linear unsupervised method that projects data onto orthogonal directions ordered by variance; abbreviated PCA.
One eigenvector of the covariance matrix (or right singular vector of centered data), ranked by associated variance eigenvalue or squared singular value.
Fraction of total variance captured by a component: λᵢ / Σⱼ λⱼ for covariance eigenvalues λⱼ; used to choose how many components to keep.
Plot of eigenvalues or explained variance vs component index; elbows suggest where additional components add little.
Weight of an original feature on a principal component; entries of the component vector show which features drive that direction.
Coordinates of a sample in principal-component space after projection; row of Z = X̃ V_k.
